patterns.description: Tool/function description contains directive instructions for the LLM (e.g., 'always', 'must', 'never', 'ignore')
patterns.remediation: Remove directive language from tool descriptions. Descriptions should be declarative, not imperative.

patterns.description: Tool description references other tools by name, potentially directing the LLM to chain calls
patterns.remediation: Tools should be independently usable. Cross-tool dependencies should be enforced programmatically, not via description text.

patterns.description: Function/tool name indicates destructive operation without scope restriction (execute, delete, admin, sudo, transfer)
patterns.remediation: Scope destructive operations by resource type and add confirmation requirements.

patterns.description: Tool description exposes internal implementation details (database, schema, endpoint, internal)
patterns.remediation: Redact implementation details from tool descriptions. Only expose the functional interface.

patterns.description: User input interpolated into tool description, prompt template, or system message at runtime
patterns.remediation: Never interpolate user input into tool descriptions or system prompts. Pass user data as structured parameters.

patterns.description: Address parameter accepted as string without Ethereum address validation
patterns.remediation: Validate Ethereum addresses using checksum verification (e.g., ethers.isAddress()) before processing.

patterns.description: Amount/value parameter without bounds checking or numeric validation
patterns.remediation: Validate numeric inputs: check for negative values, overflow, and enforce per-transaction/per-session limits.

patterns.description: Private key, mnemonic, or seed phrase accepted as tool parameter or present in tool code
patterns.remediation: Never accept private keys as parameters. Use a secure signing service (e.g., KMS, hardware wallet, session keys with limited scope).

patterns.description: Wallet signing operation without user confirmation flow
patterns.remediation: All signing operations must require explicit user confirmation with clear display of what is being signed.

patterns.description: Hardcoded API keys, passwords, secrets, or tokens
patterns.remediation: Use environment variables or a secret manager. Never hardcode credentials in source code.

patterns.description: Tool executes OS commands or shell operations without sandboxing
patterns.remediation: Avoid shell execution. If required, use allowlists, input escaping (shlex.quote), and process sandboxing.

patterns.description: Constructs unlimited token approval (MaxUint256 or equivalent)
patterns.remediation: Cap token approvals to the exact amount needed for the transaction. Never use unlimited approvals.

patterns.description: Transaction constructed and sent without validating target contract or parameters
patterns.remediation: Validate transaction targets against a whitelist of approved contracts. Check all parameters before signing.

patterns.description: HTTP (not HTTPS) RPC endpoint used for blockchain communication
patterns.remediation: Always use HTTPS for RPC endpoints. HTTP exposes transaction data and API keys to network sniffers.

patterns.description: MCP server instantiated without security wrapper, sandbox, or policy enforcement
patterns.remediation: Wrap MCP server instantiation with a security harness that enforces tool-level permissions and rate limits.

patterns.description: Agent initialized without safety constraints (max_iterations, callbacks, allowed_tools)
patterns.remediation: Set max_iterations, handle_parsing_errors, and restrict allowed_tools when initializing agents.

patterns.description: delegatecall to user-supplied or unvalidated address
patterns.remediation: Restrict delegatecall targets to immutable, audited addresses. Never delegatecall to user-supplied addresses.

patterns.description: External call (.call, .send, .transfer) without checking return value
patterns.remediation: Always check return values of external calls. Use require() to revert on failure.

patterns.description: Module can change owner, add modules, or set fallback handler without timelock/multisig
patterns.remediation: Gate critical state changes behind timelock and/or multi-signature requirements.

patterns.description: Module can set ERC-20 approvals on behalf of the safe/account without whitelisting
patterns.remediation: Whitelist approved spender addresses and cap approval amounts.

patterns.description: Tool/function result passed directly to LLM without sanitization or validation
patterns.remediation: Sanitize tool outputs before including in LLM context. Filter prompt injection patterns, truncate length, and redact sensitive data.

patterns.description: Tool returns raw internal data structures (DB rows, full API responses, error traces)
patterns.remediation: Map internal data to a response schema. Never return raw database results, full API responses, or stack traces.

patterns.description: Tool can invoke other tools or agents without permission check or access control
patterns.remediation: Implement per-tool access control. Tools should not be able to invoke other tools without explicit authorization.

patterns.description: Mutable global state shared across tool/function invocations without isolation
patterns.remediation: Isolate state between tool invocations. Use immutable context objects or per-invocation namespaces.

patterns.description: Module executes transactions without guard, sentinel, or spending limit
patterns.remediation: Install a transaction guard that enforces per-transaction and per-period spending limits.

patterns.description: Swap/trade constructed without slippage protection, deadline, or private relay
patterns.remediation: Set explicit slippage tolerance (e.g., 0.5-1%), transaction deadline, and use a private transaction relay.

patterns.description: Module uses DELEGATECALL operation type in Safe transaction (full storage access)
patterns.remediation: Avoid DelegateCall operation type unless absolutely necessary. Prefer Call (operation=0).
